column1: # Accessibility matrix rebuild (2026-04-17) for sp6: whitefish → smelt swap.
#   Smelt as prey: heavy access to cod/perch/pikeperch/flounder (forage-fish role)
#   Smelt as predator: zooplanktivore (mesozoo 0.8, macrozoo 0.6), minor piscivory
# NOTE: 0.57 is the standard OSMOSE default (Shin & Cury 2004). Not species-specific.
# Maximum ingestion rate (g food / g predator / year)
# Species-specific per literature: planktivores/small fish have higher mass-specific rates
# Palomares & Pauly (1998, doi:10.1071/mf98015) Q/B regression model
# Travers-Trolet et al. (2019, doi:10.1016/j.ecolmodel.2019.108800) OSMOSE EEC application.
# Cod rate (3.5) may need reduction to account for Contracaecum osculatum parasite effects
# on foraging capacity (Ryberg et al. 2020, doi:10.1093/conphys/coaa093).
# max = largest ratio (predator much bigger than prey)
# min = smallest ratio (predator only slightly bigger than prey)
